title: flacourtia territorialis
intro: Flacourtia territorialis is a species of plant in the family Salicaceae, commonly known as the native or New Caledonian guavaberry. It is a shrub or small tree native to New Caledonia, with edible fruit that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, thanks to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Flacourtia territorialis include infusions and decoctions of the leaves and roots, which can be used to make teas, tonics, and other remedies for various health benefits.
